finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_15.md:105-109 (Spot 1: Simone's laugh now lands behaviorally with head-shake and 'Nothing' instead of 'See, that's what I mean. You don't even know you did that'); chapters/chapter_15.md:143-153 (Spot 2: coach-speech and aphorism replaced with Simone's non-answer — 'Something like that' / silence / 'No' — removing all diagnostic explanation from this span); chapters/chapter_15.md:201-209 (Spot 3: surviving as the ONE explicit mechanism-naming, roughened from 'Being funny without knowing it' / 'Exactly' to 'The thing you do. Where you say something true and it comes out funny and you've got no idea' / 'I know'). Additionally, chapters/chapter_15.md:95 replaced near-verbatim style-bible calibration line ('I'm funnier on paper. You're funnier in person. I don't know which is worse') with 'Your voice doesn't go onto a page. I haven't figured out why.'
finding_results.notes: Four edits address the finding. (1) Spot 1: removed 'See, that's what I mean' labeling; Simone's laugh is now pure behavioral recognition — she covers her mouth, Doug asks 'What?', she shakes her head and says 'Nothing.' The accidental-funny lands through her body, not her diagnosis. (2) Spot 2: removed the coach-speech ('The minute you walk onstage with a set, the room feels you bracing for the laugh...') and replaced with Simone's silence and non-answer, which is more consistent with her style-bible profile (makes observations, does not deliver lessons). The mother transition was adjusted from 'That's the same thing my mother would say' to 'That's what my mother would do' to refer to the withholding behavior rather than a spoken maxim. The 'You need to not need' aphorism was already absent from the current chapter. (3) Spot 3: kept as the single surviving explicit diagnostic but roughened — 'Being funny without knowing it' became the more spoken-cadence 'The thing you do. Where you say something true and it comes out funny and you've got no idea'; Doug's response gained an extra beat of insistence ('I'm not being funny. I'm telling you the truth' vs. 'I'm telling you the truth'); and Simone's 'Exactly' became the flatter, less quotable 'I know.' (4) Bonus: replaced calibration example line on line 95. Across the three cited spans, Simone now names Doug's mechanism exactly once (Spot 3). No new aphorisms were introduced.

summary: Revised three dialogue spans to reduce Simone's explicit naming of Doug's accidental-funny mechanism from three instances to one. Spot 1 (line 109): replaced verbal labeling with behavioral reaction (laugh, head-shake, deflection). Spot 2 (lines 145-153): replaced coach-speech with Simone's non-answer, letting the gap where a lesson would be do the dramatic work. Spot 3 (lines 201-209): preserved as the single surviving diagnostic but roughened its cadence to sound more spoken and less quotable. Also replaced a near-verbatim style-bible calibration line on line 95. All edits preserve Simone's style-bible voice profile (dry, economical, observational rather than prescriptive), Doug's literal register, the Gloria dinner-roll transition, and the chapter's heading contract.
